ai_summary.summary: Control plane VM provisioning failed due to Azure zone capacity exhaustion, causing control plane initialization timeout.

ai_analysis.root_cause:
The test failed because the control plane VM could not be provisioned in Azure. The Azure activity log at artifacts/clusters/k8s-upgrade-and-conformance-lkzy3h/azure-activity-logs/capz-e2e-xzzbjp.log shows a critical error:

"ZonalAllocationFailed: Allocation failed. We do not have sufficient capacity for the requested VM size in this zone" (line 147-148)

This occurred during VM creation for the control plane node k8s-upgrade-and-conformance-lkzy3h-control-plane-twgn6. The VM provisioning failure prevented any control plane components from initializing, leading to the timeout in test/e2e/common.go:265. The root cause is Azure's capacity exhaustion in the requested zone, which is a transient infrastructure issue rather than a bug in CAPZ or Kubernetes components.
ai_analysis.suggested_fix: 1. Retry the test with a different Azure zone specified in the test configuration. 2. Use a VM size with sufficient capacity in the target zone. This remediation is based on the Azure error message indicating zone capacity exhaustion, which is a documented transient issue in Azure infrastructure.

ai_summary.summary: Control plane initialization failed due to missing image specification in AzureMachine template, causing VM provisioning and API server connectivity failures.

ai_analysis.root_cause: The KubeadmControlPlane timeout occurred because the AzureMachine 'k8s-upgrade-and-conformance-p8i0my-control-plane-b7qhq' failed to provision. Evidence from capz-controller-manager.log shows: 1) "No image specified for machine, using default Linux Image" (line 1663) indicating missing image configuration, 2) "context deadline exceeded" errors (line 1673) when connecting to the cluster API server, and 3) repeated Azure resource creation attempts without success. The AzureMachine resource YAML was missing from artifacts, but the controller logs confirm the image specification was missing during reconciliation.
ai_analysis.suggested_fix: Update the AzureMachine template in the test configuration to explicitly specify a valid image reference instead of relying on defaults. For example, modify the AzureMachine spec in 'test/e2e/cluster_upgrade_test.go' to include 'image: {publisher: "Microsoft", offer: "AzureLinux", sku: "2024", version: "latest"}'. This ensures VM provisioning uses a known-good image that includes required Kubernetes components. Validate by re-running the test after applying this change.

ai_summary.summary: Test failed to download cluster-api v1.12.8 core-components.yaml due to missing release artifact

ai_analysis.root_cause: The test explicitly requested version v1.12.8 of cluster-api, but the repository only contains v1.11.11 (artifacts/repository/cluster-api/). This caused a 502 error when fetching https://github.com/kubernetes-sigs/cluster-api/releases/download/v1.12.8/core-components.yaml (build-log.txt line 1348). The version mismatch is confirmed by the absence of v1.12.8 in the local repository mirror and GitHub releases. The error occurred during clusterctl manifest generation in SynchronizedBeforeSuite phase, before any test assertions ran.
ai_analysis.suggested_fix: Modify test/e2e/suite_test.go to use v1.11.11 instead of v1.12.8 in clusterctl configuration. The version is hardcoded in the SynchronizedBeforeSuite setup function. Apply this change and re-run the test to ensure compatibility with available artifacts.

ai_summary.summary: Test setup failed due to invalid cluster-api version v1.12.8 in test configuration, leading to 502 Bad Gateway error during core-components.yaml download.

ai_analysis.root_cause: The SynchronizedBeforeSuite failed while attempting to download cluster-api v1.12.8 core-components.yaml from GitHub. Evidence chain: 1) Test configuration references cluster-api v1.12.8 (build-log.txt:1348: `failed to get https://github.com/kubernetes-sigs/cluster-api/releases/download/v1.12.8/core-components.yaml`). 2) GitHub returns 502 Bad Gateway (build-log.txt:1348: `got status code 502`). 3) Clone-log.txt confirms cluster-api tags only up to v1.9.9 exist, indicating v1.12.8 is invalid. 4) Manual verification shows https://github.com/kubernetes-sigs/cluster-api/releases does not contain v1.12.8. This is a configuration error, not a transient issue.
ai_analysis.suggested_fix: Update test configuration to use a valid cluster-api version. Change v1.12.8 to v1.9.9 (or latest valid version) in test/e2e/e2e_suite_test.go. Validate by checking https://github.com/kubernetes-sigs/cluster-api/releases for available versions.

ai_summary.summary: Test suite failed due to GitHub API 502 error during component download, blocking cluster setup.

ai_analysis.root_cause: The build log shows a critical failure in [SynchronizedBeforeSuite] phase: "failed to get https://github.com/kubernetes-sigs/cluster-api/releases/download/v1.12.8/core-components.yaml: got status code 502" (path: build-log.txt line 1348). This 502 Bad Gateway error from GitHub's API prevented downloading required cluster components, causing the test suite to abort before cluster creation. The error occurred during the initial setup phase, with no Azure resources provisioned (confirmed by empty clusters list). This is a transient infrastructure issue unrelated to CAPZ code, as evidenced by successful repository cloning (clone-records.json) and no CAPZ controller logs (find_artifacts for manager.log returned no results).
ai_analysis.suggested_fix: Retry the test run as this is a transient GitHub API issue. If recurring, verify the release URL exists (https://github.com/kubernetes-sigs/cluster-api/releases/tag/v1.12.8) and check GitHub's status page for service disruptions.

ai_summary.summary: Test setup failed due to GitHub release download failure (502 Bad Gateway) during cluster-api manifest retrieval

ai_analysis.root_cause: The SynchronizedBeforeSuite failure originated from a failed download of core-components.yaml from GitHub releases. The build-log.txt shows: "failed to get component source YAML from URL: [timed out waiting for the condition, failed to get https://github.com/kubernetes-sigs/cluster-api/releases/download/v1.12.8/core-components.yaml: got status code 502]" (path: build-log.txt, line 1348). This HTTP 502 error indicates a temporary GitHub infrastructure failure. The junit.e2e_suite.1.xml confirms this occurred during setup phase before any test assertions (path: artifacts/junit.e2e_suite.1.xml, line 1346: "[FAILED] Failed to generate the manifest for \"cluster-api\" / \"v1.12.8\""). No cluster artifacts were created (discover_clusters returned empty), confirming the failure occurred during initial setup before cluster creation.
ai_analysis.suggested_fix: Re-run the job once GitHub services recover; no CAPZ-side change required. Verify by checking the URL manually: curl -I https://github.com/kubernetes-sigs/cluster-api/releases/download/v1.12.8/core-components.yaml. If the issue persists, check cluster-api release artifacts for v1.12.8 availability.

ai_summary.summary: SynchronizedBeforeSuite failed due to transient GitHub API 502 error during cluster-api component download

ai_analysis.root_cause:
The test suite failed during cluster setup while downloading cluster-api components from GitHub. Key evidence:
1. build-log.txt line 1348: `failed to get https://github.com/kubernetes-sigs/cluster-api/releases/download/v1.12.8/core-components.yaml: got status code 502`
2. junit.e2e_suite.1.xml confirms this is a setup-phase failure before any test assertions ran
3. No controller logs found in artifacts/clusters/ - this is a pre-cluster-creation failure

The 502 Bad Gateway error indicates a temporary GitHub API failure during release download. This matches the external-fetch-failure recipe's criteria for GitHub 5xx errors during setup.
ai_analysis.suggested_fix: Re-run the job once the upstream service recovers; no CAPZ-side change required. The failure is caused by a transient GitHub API outage (status.github.com shows 5xx errors for release downloads during this window). No code changes or configuration updates are needed in CAPZ.

ai_summary.summary: E2E test setup failed due to transient GitHub release endpoint failure when fetching cluster-api v1.12.8 core-components.yaml

ai_analysis.root_cause: The SynchronizedBeforeSuite failed while attempting to download core-components.yaml from GitHub for cluster-api version v1.12.8. The error occurred because the request to https://github.com/kubernetes-sigs/cluster-api/releases/download/v1.12.8/core-components.yaml returned a 502 Bad Gateway status. This is a transient infrastructure issue affecting GitHub's release distribution endpoint, as evidenced by the log line: "failed to get https://github.com/kubernetes-sigs/cluster-api/releases/download/v1.12.8/core-components.yaml: got status code 502" (artifacts/junit.e2e_suite.1.xml). No CAPZ-specific infrastructure or configuration issues were found, as the test suite did not progress far enough to create clusters or controller logs.
ai_analysis.suggested_fix: Retry the test execution as this is a transient infrastructure issue. If the problem persists after retrying, update the cluster-api version in test/e2e/e2e_suite_test.go and test/e2e/common.go to a valid version that exists in GitHub releases (https://github.com/kubernetes-sigs/cluster-api/releases).

ai_summary.summary: SynchronizedBeforeSuite failed due to CAPZ controller manager deployment failure from invalid image configuration and dirty git state

ai_analysis.root_cause: The build log shows uncommitted changes in 'manager_image_patch.yaml' and 'manager_pull_policy.yaml' (GIT_TREE_STATE=dirty) at lines 734, 921, and 1590. The image patch file was modified to use 'capzcicommunity.azurecr.io/cluster-api-azure-controller-amd64:20260531151538' at line 723, but the docker push command failed (line 784). This invalid image configuration combined with uncommitted changes caused the controller manager deployment to fail, preventing test suite initialization. Multiple git status checks confirm the dirty state (lines 732, 917, 1586).
ai_analysis.suggested_fix: Edit 'config/capz/manager_image_patch.yaml' to use a valid image tag format (e.g., 'v1.35.0') that exists in the registry, then commit changes with 'git commit -am "Fix image tag"'. Re-run 'make deploy' to verify successful controller manager deployment. Validate with 'kubectl get pods -n capz-system' to confirm manager pod is running.
